June 4, 2026
Download Biltmore Forest Connect!
The Town of Biltmore Forest is excited to announce the release of our new Town App, Biltmore Forest Connect! Designed to keep residents informed and engaged, the app serves as a convenient hub for community news, town updates, and information about local events and activities. Biltmore Forest Connect highlights the fun, social, and community events that bring neighbors together while also...

https://biltmoreforest.org/news/resident.text:
June 4, 2026
Resident Car Show - 4th of July Sign Up
After a one-year hiatus, one of our favorite community traditions is returning! The Town is excited to bring back the Car Show and invite residents to showcase the vehicles that hold a special place in their hearts. Whether it’s a classic cruiser, a modern masterpiece, a cherished family heirloom, or a unique ride with a story to tell, we want to see what makes your vehicle special. Participation...

June 2, 2026
RFQ For On-Call/As Needed Engineering Services
TOWN OF BILTMORE FOREST, NORTH CAROLINA REQUEST FOR QUALIFICATIONS FOR ON-CALL/AS-NEEDED ENGINEERING SERVICES 1. Purpose & Introduction The Town of Biltmore Forest is seeking qualified, multi-disciplinary professional engineering firms (or individuals) to provide on-call, as-needed professional civil engineering and related consulting services. https://biltmoreforest.org/news/fiscal-y.text:
May 14, 2026
Fiscal Year 2026-2027 Budget Message
The Town is pleased to present the proposed Fiscal Year 2026-2027 (FY26-27) Budget for the Town of Biltmore Forest. This budget is submitted in accordance with North Carolina General Statutes and reflects our community’s values, priorities, and resilience as we continue to recover from Hurricane Helene and invest in the future of our Town. To review the budget proposal, please click here.

https://biltmoreforest.org/news/biltmore.text:
May 11, 2026
Biltmore Forest LEADS Tick Committee Presentation (Video Recording)
On Monday, May 4, 2026, the Town of Biltmore Forest's LEADS Committee convened a Town Hall meeting to review results of tick testing throughout the Town. This information included a presentation by the research team at the University of North Carolina-Chapel Hill, the University of South Carolina, and NC State University. For a full video recording of the meeting, please click here. The meeting...

March 24, 2026
Partake is Coming to The Forest!
On Thursday, March 26th beginning at noon, we will have Partake presenting their new services that are coming to Biltmore Forest later this spring. This meeting is open to all residents of Biltmore Forest! Come and see what Partake is all about! Light refreshments will be provided as well. Partake: the app for sharing life's useful things.
